title: Pandas Study Map
subtitle: Child topic for local tabular data work in Python

nodes.label: DataFrame
nodes.definition: A two-dimensional table-like data structure with rows and columns.
nodes.whyItMatters: DataFrame is the main object used for most Pandas data cleaning and transformation work.
nodes.safeSentence: A DataFrame is the main Pandas table object.

note.summary: Most Pandas workflows start by loading or creating a DataFrame.

nodes.label: Series
nodes.definition: A one-dimensional labeled array, often representing one column from a DataFrame.
nodes.whyItMatters: Understanding Series helps explain how Pandas handles columns and vectorized operations.
nodes.safeSentence: A Series is like one labeled column of data.

note.summary: A DataFrame is made of Series-like columns.

nodes.label: GroupBy
nodes.definition: A split-apply-combine operation used to group data and calculate summaries.
nodes.whyItMatters: GroupBy is how raw rows become useful totals, averages, counts, and grouped metrics.
nodes.safeSentence: GroupBy turns detailed rows into grouped summaries.

note.summary: GroupBy is one of the most important Pandas patterns for reporting and analytics.

paths.label: Pandas Core Path
paths.description: DataFrames contain Series-like columns, and GroupBy turns rows into useful summaries.
paths.rehearsalPrompt: Explain DataFrame, Series, and GroupBy in simple words.
